User Story: As an author, I can open the app and have the editor load to a ready state so I can start building.
Expected Behavior: Loader veil (#loader) shows the building message; boot() resolves the world by priority (mod -> worldpack -> sample -> saved -> default), grounds the player, and instantiates WorldEditor; markWorldReady() removes #loader and sets window.__WORLD_READY__=true and body[data-world-ready]=true with no console errors; on failure boot().catch sets the loader subtitle to a failure message.
Source Evidence: src/main.js:1101 (boot); src/main.js:1278 (markWorldReady); src/main.js:1409 (error handler); index.html:204 (#loader)
UI/API Entry Point: index.html -> /src/main.js (default editor mode, no query params)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-001. Boot failure surfaces only as a loader subtitle (no dismissable error UI) -> candidate UX defect for Phase E.

User Story: As an author, I can click Play to launch the current world in play mode so I can test it as a player.
Expected Behavior: Clicking #enter-play flushes any pending autosave, then navigates to /?play=1; play mode adds body.play-mode (CSS hides the editor controls hint), shows the arrival controls hint and the #exit-play button, and wires runtime systems instead of the editor.
Source Evidence: index.html:190 (#enter-play); src/main.js:1238 (click -> flushAutosave -> /?play=1); src/main.js:70-73 (playMode flag + body.play-mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-002. test:editor-ux1 covers 9 candidate stories; per-story coverage adequacy to be audited in Phase T.

User Story: As a player in play mode, I can click the Editor button to return to the authoring surface.
Expected Behavior: #exit-play is hidden by default and shown only when body has play-mode (CSS); clicking it navigates to / (editor mode). The editor toolbar (#toolbar) is hidden in runtime/play mode.
Source Evidence: index.html:179-180,195 (#exit-play CSS + button); src/main.js:1192 (exit-play click -> location='/'); src/main.js:1187 (toolbar hidden in runtime)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-003.

User Story: As a player, I can move with WASD/arrows relative to the camera and sprint with Shift while staying grounded on the terrain.
Expected Behavior: getMoveAxis maps WASD/arrows to forward/strafe; motion is built from the camera yaw so W always goes where the camera faces; walkSpeed 8.5, sprintSpeed 15 while ShiftLeft/Right is held; horizontal velocity is damped (accel 13); horizontal collisions are resolved by ColliderSystem; the player stays grounded via terrain/collider support height each frame.
Source Evidence: src/player/PlayerController.js:35-64 (move/sprint); src/core/input.js:53 (getMoveAxis); src/player/PlayerController.js:99 (_groundHeight)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-004. first-playable-proof drives movement via __PLAYER_MOVE_DO__ (input-equivalent hold/step).

User Story: As a player, I can press Space to jump from the ground and land back safely on the terrain.
Expected Behavior: When grounded and Space wasPressed -> velocityY = jumpSpeed (8.5) and grounded=false; gravity 26 integrates velocityY each frame; when position.y <= groundY the player snaps to groundY with velocityY=0 and grounded=true. Space is preventDefault'd so the page never scrolls.
Source Evidence: src/player/PlayerController.js:68-81 (jump+gravity+land); src/core/input.js:92-95 (Space preventDefault)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-005.

User Story: As a player, I can look with the mouse (pointer-locked), release with Esc, and toggle first/third person with V.
Expected Behavior: Clicking the canvas requests pointer lock; while locked, mouse deltas drive yaw and clamped pitch (minPitch -1.15, maxPitch 0.85); Esc releases the lock (browser); KeyV toggleMode flips first/third and shows/hides the player mesh; a centre crosshair shows only in first-person while the pointer is locked.
Source Evidence: src/player/PlayerCameraController.js:42-62 (look + toggle); src/core/input.js:71-73,117-124 (pointer lock); src/main.js:1382-1383 (crosshair)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-006.

User Story: As a developer/author, I can toggle a debug HUD (backquote) and a performance-budget HUD (B) to inspect runtime stats.
Expected Behavior: DebugPanel renders fps/camera/draw-calls/vegetation/player stats and toggles via Backquote (debug.toggle); BudgetHUD shows green/yellow/red budget status, samples already-computed counters at ~4 Hz into a reused scratch, and toggles via KeyB; both default visible while authoring and hidden in runtime; BudgetHUD is DEV-only (stripped from production builds).
Source Evidence: src/debug/DebugPanel.js:34 (toggle); src/debug/BudgetHUD.js:26 (constructor/throttle); src/main.js:1316-1317 (Backquote/KeyB toggles); src/main.js:1033 (DEV-only construction)
UI/API Entry Point: Backquote / KeyB keys in runtime; HUDs visible by default in editor
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-007 (candidate US-077 performance HUD shares BudgetHUD). BudgetHUD absent in production builds.

User Story: As an author, I can create a new world and have any loaded document validated/normalized so invalid data cannot corrupt runtime state.
Expected Behavior: createWorldDocument builds a v2 document (alpine terrain default; grass/trees/bushes/water/atmosphere/wildlife/lighting configs; empty objects/objectives/enemies/encounters). validateWorldDocument migrates v1/legacy saves, applies safe defaults on an unknown version, allow-lists primitives/object-types/camera-mode/collider-types, caps placed objects at MAX_PLACED_OBJECTS (20000), and returns {document, warnings}; it never throws on bad fields (replaces with defaults and pushes a warning).
Source Evidence: src/world/WorldDocument.js:17 (createWorldDocument); src/world/WorldValidation.js:37 (validateWorldDocument); src/world/WorldValidation.js:27 (MAX_PLACED_OBJECTS)
UI/API Entry Point: src/world API: new-world default + validation on every load/save/import
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-008. Validation is permissive (console warnings only); silent normalization is a candidate UX gap for Phase E.

User Story: As an author, I can save my world and have it reload automatically so my work persists across sessions.
Expected Behavior: save() validates the document (stamping metadata.updatedAt) and writes JSON to localStorage key grass-world-builder-save; load() reads that key, returns null when absent, else JSON.parse + validateWorldDocument -> {document, warnings}; boot() uses load() as the 4th-priority world source.
Source Evidence: src/world/WorldSerializer.js:9 (save); src/world/WorldSerializer.js:21 (load); src/world/WorldDocument.js:15 (WORLD_STORAGE_KEY); src/main.js:1139 (boot uses load)
UI/API Entry Point: src/world API: editor autosave + boot load
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-009. load() calls JSON.parse(raw) with no try/catch -> a corrupted localStorage value throws unhandled at boot; candidate Data/State defect (High) for Phase E.

User Story: As an author, I can export my world to a .world.json file and import a world file back, with validation and identity preserved.
Expected Behavior: exportWorldDocument validates the document and downloads <slug(name)>.world.json as a pretty-printed JSON blob via an object URL; importWorldDocumentFile reads a File via FileReader, JSON.parses it, runs validateWorldDocument, resolves {document, warnings}, and rejects on parse/read error.
Source Evidence: src/world/WorldExport.js:3 (exportWorldDocument); src/world/WorldExport.js:17 (importWorldDocumentFile)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-010. WorldExport API confirmed first-hand; the WorldEditor button wiring (src/editor/WorldEditor.js import/export handlers) is NOT yet line-verified -> Code Confidence Med. Editor-level import error surfaces as a label only (candidate UX gap).

Feature Name: Select objects from the hierarchy outliner
User Story: As an author, I can select world objects from a sectioned hierarchy list so I can find and edit them without hunting in the viewport.
Expected Behavior: HierarchyPanel.render lists placed/generated objects (+ read-only weapons/objective/authoring) in sections with live counts; clicking a selectable row fires onSelect (replace) and Shift/Ctrl/Cmd-click fires onToggleSelect (additive); selection is two-way (viewport picks re-render rows highlighted); empty world shows the place-an-object prompt; each section caps at 200 rows and surfaces the rest as a +N more line.
Source Evidence: src/editor/HierarchyPanel.js:49 (render); src/editor/HierarchyPanel.js:104-107 (click->select/toggle); src/editor/HierarchyPanel.js:20,75-81 (200-cap + +N more); src/editor/SelectionGroup.js:61 (set)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-012. 200-rows/section cap hides overflow behind +N more (honest but no search/filter) - candidate UX gap for Phase E.

User Story: As an author, I can choose an asset and click the terrain to place a grounded, selectable object.
Expected Behavior: With an asset armed, a terrain click resolves the hit point, applies snap (snap.snapPlacement), creates the object via the manager, and records an AddObjectsCommand (undoable); placement marks the world dirty (schedules autosave) and refreshes the outliner. _placeAssetAt(x,z) is the single placement path.
Source Evidence: src/editor/WorldEditor.js:1063 (click->_placeAssetAt); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:1069-1073 (_placeAssetAt + snap + AddObjectsCommand); src/assets/AssetLibrary.js (asset source)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor viewport click with an armed asset (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-013.

User Story: As an author, I can transform a selected object with the gizmo or by typing exact position/rotation/scale, and the change is undoable.
Expected Behavior: The TransformControls gizmo drag records a TransformObjectsCommand on drag-end (before/after states); TransformInspector shows the primary selection's pos/rotation(deg)/scale, commits on change/Enter (rotation deg->rad), and is disabled when 0 or >1 objects are selected; typed edits go through the same TransformObjectsCommand so undo + autosave fire; invalid numeric input falls back to the current value.
Source Evidence: src/editor/TransformInspector.js:44 (setObject/disable); src/editor/TransformInspector.js:71-83 (_commit deg->rad); src/editor/TransformInspector.js:108-110 (commit on change/Enter); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:216 (drag-end TransformObjectsCommand)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-014. Numeric inspector is single-primary only (disabled on multi-select) by design.

User Story: As an author, I can select multiple objects and transform them together about a shared pivot.
Expected Behavior: SelectionGroup holds the selected objects without reparenting (world-correct for serialization), draws a BoxHelper per object, centres a pivot at the selection centroid (recenterPivot), and applies a delta-matrix group transform during drag (beginDrag/applyDrag/endDrag) preserving relative offsets; grass/tree/collider rebuild happens once on drag-end (returns before+after boxes); primary = most-recently added drives single-object UI.
Source Evidence: src/editor/SelectionGroup.js:88 (recenterPivot); src/editor/SelectionGroup.js:102-132 (begin/apply/endDrag delta-matrix); src/editor/SelectionGroup.js:136-150 (BoxHelpers)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor viewport additive selection (Shift/Ctrl) + gizmo (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-015.

User Story: As an author, I can duplicate or delete the selected objects, and both are undoable.
Expected Behavior: Duplicate (_duplicateSelected) clones each source via manager.duplicate and records one AddObjectsCommand; Delete (_deleteSelected) records a RemoveObjectsCommand via history.execute (do() detaches/parks the objects); the Delete/Backspace key also triggers delete; RemoveObjectsCommand.undo re-attaches the exact same instance (object retention) and dispose() frees only parked objects.
Source Evidence: src/editor/WorldEditor.js:1253-1262 (_duplicateSelected); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:1132-1139 (_deleteSelected); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:461-462,943 (buttons + Delete/Backspace key); src/editor/commands/WorldObjectCommands.js:17,43 (Add/Remove commands)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor Duplicate/Delete buttons + Delete/Backspace key (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-016.

User Story: As an author, I can undo and redo my changes with Ctrl/Cmd+Z (Shift+Z or Y to redo) or the Undo/Redo buttons.
Expected Behavior: CommandStack (limit 100) records commands via execute()/push(); undo() pops -> command.undo() -> pushes to redo; redo() re-applies; a new action discards the redo branch and disposes parked objects; eviction past the limit disposes the oldest. WorldEditor binds KeyZ->undo, Shift+KeyZ / KeyY->redo, and Undo/Redo buttons; onChange refreshes the buttons and marks the world dirty.
Source Evidence: src/editor/CommandStack.js:45-96 (execute/push/undo/redo/_record); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:160 (CommandStack); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:929-936 (KeyZ/Shift+Z/KeyY); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:482-483,1142-1147 (buttons + _undo/_redo)
UI/API Entry Point: Ctrl/Cmd+Z, Shift+Z, Y + Undo/Redo buttons (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-017.

Feature Name: Grid snapping for transforms and placement
User Story: As an author, I can enable grid snapping so transforms and placements land on a regular grid.
Expected Behavior: SnapSettings (default OFF; grid 0.5m, rot 15deg, scale 0.25) snaps via snapToGrid/snapVec3; applyTo pushes translation/rotation/scale snap into TransformControls (or clears it when off); snapPlacement snaps new-object placement points when enabled; snapping is an editor authoring aid only - it never changes the serialized document schema.
Source Evidence: src/editor/SnapSettings.js:22-40 (snap math); src/editor/SnapSettings.js:63-69 (applyTo); src/editor/SnapSettings.js:77-80 (snapPlacement); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:164,225,1016,1071 (SnapSettings wiring)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor snap toggle in sidebar (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-018. Editor-only; never serialized.

User Story: As an author, I can hide and lock editor layers to focus and to prevent stray edits.
Expected Behavior: LayerModel owns hidden/locked Sets over EDITOR_LAYERS (objects/terrain/water/wildlife/ambient/weapons); setVisible fires onVisibility(id,visible) only on a real change (editor performs the scene toggle); a locked layer gates viewport selection (isObjectInLockedLayer); reset() restores all visible+unlocked on world load. CRITICAL: editor-session-only view state - NEVER written to the WorldDocument, so it can never hide content for a player.
Source Evidence: src/editor/LayerModel.js:21-28 (EDITOR_LAYERS); src/editor/LayerModel.js:79-87 (setVisible); src/editor/LayerModel.js:110-122 (lock gates selection + reset); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:165,494 (LayerModel + LayersPanel)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-019. Session-only; never persisted (verified in code).

User Story: As an author, my edits autosave shortly after I stop so I do not lose work, with a visible status.
Expected Behavior: EditorAutosave runs idle->dirty->saving->saved|error; markDirty() arms a 1500ms debounce so an edit burst coalesces to one save; flush() saves immediately (before entering Play); reset() drops pending work on world load; the injected save fn persists via the editor serialize+localStorage path; failures set status=error + lastError. WorldEditor._markDirty() (called from every mutation incl. history onChange) drives it; flushAutosave() runs on the Play button.
Source Evidence: src/editor/EditorAutosave.js:11,57-70 (debounce/flush/reset); src/editor/EditorAutosave.js:79-90 (_run + error); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:167,1456-1457,1464 (EditorAutosave + _markDirty + flushAutosave)
UI/API Entry Point: Automatic (debounced) after edits; flush on Play (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-020. Save error surfaces as a status label only - candidate UX gap for Phase E.

Feature Name: Configure collider proxy on an object
User Story: As an author, I can configure an object's collider proxy (type + grass/tree exclusion) so it blocks the player and clears vegetation appropriately.
Expected Behavior: ColliderInspector offers a type select (none/box/cylinder/plane-walkable/ramp/trigger) + a Wire debug toggle; changing the type fires onChange with {type, excludeGrass} which the editor writes to object.userData.collider and marks dirty; defaultColliderForAsset derives a per-asset-kind default; normalizeCollider clamps to the allow-list; validateWorldDocument allow-lists collider types on load/save so a bad collider cannot corrupt runtime; ColliderSystem consumes the proxy for player collision.
Source Evidence: src/editor/ColliderInspector.js:12,31-36 (type select); src/editor/ColliderInspector.js:54-60 (onChange {type,excludeGrass}); src/physics/ColliderProxy.js:12-45 (default/normalize/types); src/editor/WorldEditor.js:535-544 (ColliderInspector wiring -> _markDirty)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-021.

Feature Name: Import GLB asset with budget gate
User Story: As an author, I can import a .glb/.gltf model into the asset library, and an over-budget model is refused before it is stored.
Expected Behavior: importGLTF parses the GLB (GLTFLoader), extracts animation metadata, computes a per-asset budget (triangles/materials/textures/nodes/maxDimension) and grades it ok/warn/reject; a reject verdict throws AssetBudgetError BEFORE storeAsset (the asset never reaches IndexedDB or the in-memory map); a warn asset is stored but flagged (budget.severity=warn); stored metadata holds bounds, default collider/exclusion, animation and budget; the binary lives in IndexedDB (reference-based, never embedded in the world doc).
Source Evidence: src/assets/AssetImporter.js:13-48 (importGLTF + reject-before-store); src/assets/AssetBudget.js:22-29 (limits); src/assets/AssetBudget.js:48-90 (computeAssetBudget)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-022. Reject-before-store verified in code; browser proof asserts capture + non-vacuous reject + persist-as-reference; pure-Node test:asset-pipeline covers the budget math.

Feature Name: Import image and build a 2D->3D relief asset
User Story: As an author, I can import an image or draw on a canvas and turn its luminance into an orbitable 3D relief asset I can place in the world.
Expected Behavior: ReliefAssetTool opens its own WebGL viewport; the source raster's luminance becomes height and its color becomes per-vertex color (heightScale/segments/smoothing/invert dials), producing a relief mesh; onCreateAsset hands the geometry to AssetImporter.importRelief which stores it (relief mime, bounds, default collider) and caches the geometry. Plain image import (importImage) stores an image asset with a default 4x3 plane bounds.
Source Evidence: src/editor/ReliefAssetTool.js:14-51 (tool open + dials, luminance->height); src/assets/AssetImporter.js:50-67 (importImage); src/assets/AssetImporter.js:69-89 (importRelief)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor Relief tool + image import -> AssetLibrary
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-023. test:asset-pipeline-proof centers on GLB import; the relief-tool draw->mesh path is not separately asserted - Phase T coverage check.

User Story: As an author, I can save a selection of objects as a reusable prefab and place it into the world, with placed copies independent of the template.
Expected Behavior: PrefabLibrary.createFromObjects builds a prefab from serialized world-object descriptors (prefabFromWorldObjects), persists it (user tier) and de-dupes ids; PrefabInstancer.instantiate expands a prefab to normal world-object descriptors (each carrying prefabRef) via the manager, snapping the prefab origin to terrain height while preserving child offsets; deleting a prefab template never touches already-placed objects; a missing prefab falls back to a primitive.
Source Evidence: src/prefabs/PrefabLibrary.js:69-82 (createFromObjects + persist); src/prefabs/PrefabLibrary.js:98-106 (delete leaves placed objects); src/prefabs/PrefabInstancer.js:28-47 (instantiate + terrain snap)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor Prefab panel: save selection / place (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-024. test:prefabgen asserts prefab-backed placement (prefabRef) + builtin catalog + missing-prefab fallback; user-prefab SAVE (createFromObjects from a selection) is NOT directly asserted - Phase T coverage check.

User Story: As an author, I can browse a catalog of built-in kits and my saved prefabs to choose what to place.
Expected Behavior: PrefabLibrary.list returns built-ins first (system kits regenerated each init via createBuiltinPrefabs, sorted by name, never deletable/persisted) then user prefabs most-recent-first; get/has/isBuiltin resolve across both tiers; rename/delete/setThumbnail operate on user prefabs only; importManifest merges previously-unknown prefabs from a world manifest (existing kept).
Source Evidence: src/prefabs/PrefabLibrary.js:42-63 (two-tier list/get/isBuiltin); src/prefabs/PrefabLibrary.js:36-39 (builtins via createBuiltinPrefabs); src/prefabs/PrefabLibrary.js:136-146 (importManifest)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-025. Candidate's test:genlib is the GENERATOR-library proof (mis-targeted); test:prefabgen only INDIRECTLY confirms the builtin catalog is listed (builtin-hut dropdown). No direct browse/list/rename/delete UI assertion - Phase T: add a prefab-library test or mark Deferred.

Feature Name: Import and export data-only mod packages
User Story: As an author, I can export my world as a data-only mod package and import a mod package, without any code execution and without clobbering my existing content.
Expected Behavior: ModExporter.buildWorldMod wraps the world in a worldpack (assets embedded once) + user prefab defs + builtin kit references; downloadModPackage/Zip emit .modpack.json/.zip. ModRegistry.install validates the package (refuses invalid), imports assets->AssetLibrary and prefabs->PrefabLibrary SKIPPING id collisions (never overwrites user content), records a provenance entry; NO code is ever executed; getModWorld returns an installed mod's world for the ?mod= play path; uninstall removes the entry but leaves contributed assets/prefabs.
Source Evidence: src/mods/ModRegistry.js:53-133 (install + collision-skip + no-code-exec); src/mods/ModRegistry.js:136-140 (getModWorld); src/mods/ModExporter.js:21-33 (buildWorldMod); src/mods/ModExporter.js:112-123 (download); src/main.js:1110-1115 (?mod= load)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-026. NO-AUTOMATED-COVERAGE: no mod test exists; candidate's test:asset-pipeline is the pure-Node budget regression and does NOT exercise mods. Phase T MUST write a mod import/export test or mark Deferred. Security-relevant (untrusted import): install validates + skips id collisions + executes no code (verified in code).

User Story: As an author, I can generate a deterministic procedural city of roads, buildings, props and a landmark that I can then select, lock, or regenerate.
Expected Behavior: The Procedural panel generate() reads config (seed/style/blocks/density) and calls generateGeneratorObjects(city,...) -> generateCityLayout (seeded mulberry32 over seed:style -> identical layout per seed) -> cityLayoutToWorldObjects emitting NORMAL WorldObjects tagged layoutRole (path/building/vegetation/landmark) with a generatorId; counts hard-capped (roads 200/buildings 400/props 300, emitter total 1500); roads are receive-only (no shadow cast); the instance is recorded in document.generators.instances; Lock nulls generatorId (objects become permanent); regenerate picks a fresh seed.
Source Evidence: src/generators/CityLayout.js:14-15 (seeded layout); src/generators/cityEmitter.js:31 (emit roles); src/generators/GeneratorRegistry.js:44-57,145 (registry + generateGeneratorObjects); src/editor/ProceduralPanel.js:39-62 (generate)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-027. Generator internals confirmed via code trace; registry + generate() verified first-hand.

User Story: As an author, I can generate camp, ruin, and forest instances that coexist independently, each deterministic per seed.
Expected Behavior: Each type has a seeded layout + emitter registered in GENERATORS: camp emits tents (building) ringed around a fire (landmark) + crates (prop) + DATA-ONLY gameplay markers (spawn/trigger/sign/pickup) selectable like objects; ruin emits toppled walls (edge)/columns (prop)/rubble/central platform + sign; forest emits trees (trunk+canopy vegetation, kept atomic against the cap) + rocks; all deterministic via mulberry32(seed:style); tents are prefab-backed (builtin hut) with primitive fallback; Lock detaches a type's objects; multiple instances coexist via distinct generatorIds.
Source Evidence: src/generators/CampGenerator.js:34,110; src/generators/RuinGenerator.js:20,88; src/generators/ForestGenerator.js:24,67; src/generators/GeneratorRegistry.js:58 (registry entries)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-028. test:genlib (Generator Library v1) asserts camp/ruin/forest coexist, data-only camp markers, deterministic regenerate, prefab fallback.

Feature Name: Generate roads, plazas, and connectors linking anchors
User Story: As an author, I can generate roads and plazas, and a connector that links two generator instances by their origins.
Expected Behavior: A connector has NO origin - it is defined by two anchors (from/to resolved from generator instances via landmarkAnchors); generateConnectorLayout (seeded over seed:style:from->to) emits road-plane path segments (layoutRole path) via emitRoadPath + endpoint marker posts; the road generator emits path/avenue/crossroad widths + lamp posts; the plaza emits paving + central landmark + props + spawn/sign/trigger markers; the panel's anchor source slots list other generator instances to link; Lock detaches the connector only.
Source Evidence: src/generators/ConnectorGenerator.js:19,59; src/generators/RoadGenerator.js:33,85; src/generators/PlazaGenerator.js:23,70; src/generators/landmarkAnchors.js:10,20 (anchor resolve/list); src/editor/ProceduralPanel.js:119-120 (usesOrigin false)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-029.

Feature Name: Author and traverse a coherent settlement (layout QA)
User Story: As an author, I can compose a connected settlement (camp+plaza+city linked by a connector) that is readable and traversable - the player spawns clear of solids and can see a landmark.
Expected Behavior: The layout QA gate builds the real scene graph for village/camp/plaza/city and asserts: round-trip role stability; per-generator object cap; no building overlaps (validatePlacement, 25% vol tol); no path through a building (>15% footprint); valid markers (spawn/sign/trigger/pickup/door); >=1 landmark; a center within 12u of each cluster origin; spawn clearance (no solid within 2.5u); spawn sees the nearest landmark within 260u with line-of-sight; a connector links two distinct cluster origins within 30u. Runtime __LAYOUT_DEBUG__ exposes spawn/landmarks/role counts/instanced batches for the browser proof.
Source Evidence: scripts/layout-gates.mjs:31-36 (thresholds); scripts/layout-gates.mjs:155-282 (assertions); src/generators/PlacementValidator.js:19 (validatePlacement); src/main.js:609-627 (__LAYOUT_DEBUG__)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor compose + Play; deterministic gate qa:layout + browser proof
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-030. qa:layout is the deterministic Node gate; test:settlement-layout is the in-browser proof reading __LAYOUT_DEBUG__.

User Story: As an author, I can create splines, masks, and modifiers whose derived visuals (e.g. a beacon trail) rebuild from the document each load and are fully undoable.
Expected Behavior: The authoring block (splines/masks/modifiers) is the source of truth; AuthoringRuntime.load/rebuild derives one THREE.Group per enabled modifier - markers as a single InstancedMesh + the mask edge as a Torus (deriveBeaconTrail), runs in BOTH editor preview and play, idempotent load/clear/dispose, never baked into objects; a dangling spline reference is skipped (no crash). Editor edits go through Add/Remove/UpdateAuthoringItemCommand (pure-data mutations of doc.authoring + runtime.rebuild), undoable; the spline/mask edit gizmos are editor-only.
Source Evidence: src/world/authoring/AuthoringRuntime.js:27-51 (load/rebuild derived); src/world/authoring/AuthoringRuntime.js:68-111 (_buildModifier markers+ring); src/editor/commands/AuthoringCommands.js:17-99 (Add/Remove/Update authoring commands)
UI/API Entry Point: Editor Authoring panel + spline/mask edit tools (editor mode)
Notes: Supersedes candidate US-031.
